message:
fix(quic): add packet send-state guard and fix congestion recovery epoch

Two transport-level correctness fixes in the native QUIC stack:

1. Packet send-state guard (connection.rs): add ensure_packet_send_state()
   called before every on_packet_sent, rejecting sends on closed
   connections and application-data packets before 1-RTT is established.
   Prevents illegal packet transmission that could confuse the peer.
   Two tests verify both error conditions.

2. Congestion recovery epoch (transport.rs): fix detect_and_remove_lost_packets
   to key the recovery epoch off the newest lost packet's send time
   rather than current wall-clock time (now_micros). This prevents
   spurious duplicate cwnd reductions when late ACK processing reports
   older losses that were already accounted for.
   Test verifies older lost packets don't trigger extra reductions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
